subject: Ecology
title: Island Gigantism and Dwarfism
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Island Gigantism and Dwarfism.

hook.text: Islands shrink elephants and enlarge lizards—isolation plays tricks with body size.

buildup.text: Large mammals on islands often evolve smaller bodies because resources are limited.

discovery.text: Small animals often grow larger—Komodo dragons and giant tortoises are island giants.

twist.text: Dwarf elephants the size of ponies once lived on Mediterranean islands alongside giant swans.

climax.text: The island rule—big things shrink, small things grow—has been documented across hundreds of species.

punchline.text: Islands bend the rules of body size.

quiz.question: What is the island rule?
options: Large species shrink and small species grow on islands
Body size stays the same on islands
